title: ANSI Color Output: Auto-Disable on Pipe + NO_COLOR
desc: CLI tool ANSI color implementation: auto-disable on pipe/redirect + NO_COLOR env var support. Prevents garbage escape codes in logs/scripts.
steps: Create Ansi.cs helper: static readonly bool IsEnabled = !Console.IsOutputRedirected && Environment.GetEnvironmentVariable("NO_COLOR") == null;
Apply colors: Ansi.IsEnabled ? $"\x1b[1;96m{text}\x1b[0m" : text (always provide plain fallback)
Color palette: dim cyan \x1b[2;36m = headers, bold cyan \x1b[1;96m = key values, bold green \x1b[1;32m = OK, bold red \x1b[1;31m = MISS/error, yellow \x1b[33m = warnings
IsEnabled is static readonly — evaluated once at startup, never changes mid-process
Test: redirect stdout to file and verify no escape codes appear in output
author: claude
